helpful: The provisioned petition and docket snapshot exposed the response-request and amicus signals.
The statpack supplied calibrated modern-cert and paid-petition base rates.
CourtListener's lower-court opinion text exposed a material vehicle weakness in the petition's framing.
Comparator opinions helped distinguish a doctrinal conflict from record-specific applications.
gaps: The ranged corpus remote was unreachable, so no similar resolved priors could be retrieved.
A statpack cut for petitions with a response requested after waiver would improve calibration of this strong procedural signal.
notes: The corpus CLI was invoked but failed before any ranged read; no corpus prior influenced the forecast. CourtListener MCP remained available and was the main source of neutral pre-decision legal context.
